extends.description: The Acme Enterprise Extension builds on the core Acme Design System, adding enterprise-specific components, stricter accessibility requirements, and product-specific token overrides for the enterprise product suite.

purpose.description: The Enterprise Extension provides additional components, tokens, and guidelines required by Acme's enterprise product line — admin dashboards, data-heavy workflows, and compliance-sensitive interfaces. It inherits the full core system and layers enterprise-specific concerns on top.

useCases.description: When building an enterprise product within the Acme suite that requires data tables, advanced form patterns, or admin-specific UI.
When building a consumer-facing product that does not need enterprise-specific components or compliance constraints.

alternative.name: Acme Core Design System
alternative.rationale: The core system provides everything needed for consumer products. The enterprise extension adds weight and complexity (additional tokens, stricter rules) that are unnecessary outside the enterprise product line.

bestPractices.guidance: Always install the core design system as a peer dependency alongside the enterprise extension. Do not use the extension without the core.
bestPractices.rationale: The extension inherits components, tokens, and guidelines from the core. Without the core installed, token references will be unresolved and inherited components will be missing.

bestPractices.guidance: When the enterprise extension provides an extended version of a core component (e.g., enterprise Button), use the extension's version. Do not import both the core and extension versions of the same component.
bestPractices.rationale: Extended components are designed to be drop-in replacements. Importing both creates ambiguity about which version is in use and can cause style conflicts.

bestPractices.guidance: Enterprise products must meet WCAG 2.2 AA as a minimum. Components inherited from the core already meet this bar — do not weaken their accessibility behavior.
bestPractices.rationale: Enterprise customers include government agencies and regulated industries that contractually require AA compliance. Weakening inherited accessibility behavior (e.g., removing aria attributes, reducing contrast) violates these contracts.

documentation.name: Enterprise Components
documentation.description: Components that extend or specialize core components for enterprise use cases, plus net-new enterprise-only components.

metadata.value: The enterprise Button extends the core Button with an additional 'enterprise' variant for admin interfaces, a 'theme' prop for sub-brand switching, and stricter content guidelines. All core anatomy, states, accessibility behavior, and base variants (primary, secondary, ghost, danger) are inherited without modification.
action

extends.name: button
extends.description: Extends the core Button with an 'enterprise' variant, a 'theme' prop for sub-brand theming, and stricter content guidelines for enterprise compliance.

modifications.description: A new high-emphasis variant using the enterprise brand color. Used for primary actions in admin interfaces where the standard primary blue conflicts with status indicators.
A 'theme' prop accepting 'default' or 'admin' that switches the button's token set to the admin sub-brand palette.
Added enterprise-specific content guidelines requiring action verbs from an approved terminology list for compliance with enterprise UX writing standards.
Anatomy (container, label, icon, focus-ring) inherited from core without changes.
All accessibility requirements inherited from core. The enterprise extension does not weaken any WCAG compliance behavior.

items.name: emphasis
items.description: Extends the core emphasis variants with an enterprise-specific option.

values.name: primary
values.description: Inherited from core. High-emphasis — the main action on the surface. Uses a solid, filled background.

values.name: secondary
values.description: Inherited from core. Medium-emphasis — important but not the primary action. Uses a visible border and transparent background.

values.name: ghost
values.description: Inherited from core. Low-emphasis — tertiary actions, toolbar actions, or dense layouts. No background or border in the default state.

values.name: danger
values.description: Inherited from core. High-emphasis destructive — signals an irreversible action.

values.name: enterprise
values.description: Added by extension. A high-emphasis variant using the enterprise teal palette. Used for primary actions in admin dashboards where the standard primary blue conflicts with informational status indicators.

useCases.description: When the primary action sits alongside blue informational badges or status indicators in admin interfaces, and the standard primary blue would not be visually distinct.
When building consumer-facing UI that uses the standard Acme brand palette.

alternative.name: primary
alternative.rationale: The enterprise variant's teal color is specific to the admin sub-brand. Consumer products use the standard primary blue.

properties.name: theme
properties.description: Switches the button's token set to the admin sub-brand palette. When set to 'admin', the button uses enterprise-specific color tokens for all variants. This prop is additive — it does not change the core API surface.

variants.name: enterprise
variants.description: Enterprise teal variant — used in admin interfaces.

items.guidance: Use action verbs from the enterprise approved terminology list for all button labels in admin interfaces.
items.rationale: Enterprise products are subject to UX writing reviews. Approved terminology ensures consistency across the admin suite and reduces review cycles.

items.guidance: Do not use the enterprise variant outside of admin interfaces. Use the standard primary variant for all other contexts.
items.rationale: The enterprise teal color is tied to the admin sub-brand. Using it in consumer or marketing contexts violates the brand guidelines and creates visual inconsistency.

agents.intent: Trigger a user-initiated action within the current view without causing page navigation.

constraints.rule: Do not use for navigating to a different page or URL.
Limit each surface to one primary-emphasis button.
Always provide an accessible label via visible text or aria-label.

disambiguation.distinction: Use button for in-page actions; use link for navigation to a URL.
Use button when a visible text label is present; use icon-button for icon-only affordances.

antiPatterns.description: Using a button to navigate to another page.
antiPatterns.instead: Use a link element with href.

antiPatterns.description: Placing multiple primary buttons on the same surface.
antiPatterns.instead: Use one primary and one or more secondary or tertiary buttons.

metadata.value: A high-performance, virtualized data grid for displaying and interacting with large tabular datasets. This component is enterprise-only — it has no equivalent in the core design system. It composes core components (Button, Checkbox, Badge, Tooltip) for its internal UI.
data-display

useCases.description: When displaying datasets with more than 50 rows that require sorting, filtering, pagination, or inline editing.
When displaying a simple list of fewer than 20 items without sorting or filtering needs.

alternative.name: data-table
alternative.rationale: The core data-table component is lighter weight and more appropriate for simple tabular displays. The data grid's virtualization and advanced features add unnecessary complexity for small datasets.

agents.intent: Clarify when to use DataGrid versus a simple table or list.

properties.name: columns
properties.description: Column definitions specifying header labels, data accessors, sort behavior, filter type, and cell renderers.

properties.name: data
properties.description: The dataset to display. Accepts an array of objects where each object represents a row. The grid virtualizes rendering — only visible rows are in the DOM.

properties.name: pageSize
properties.description: Number of rows per page. When set, the grid displays pagination controls.

properties.name: selectable
properties.description: When true, each row displays a checkbox for selection. A header checkbox toggles select-all.

events.name: onSort
events.description: Fires when a column header is clicked to change sort order.

events.name: onSelectionChange
events.description: Fires when the row selection changes.

documentation.name: Enterprise Tokens
documentation.description: Tokens that extend or override core tokens for the enterprise product line.

metadata.value: The enterprise action color used for primary interactive elements in admin dashboards. Replaces the core primary blue (#0055b3) with enterprise teal (#00796b) to avoid conflict with informational blue indicators common in data-heavy admin UI.
semantic
The enterprise primary action color — teal, replacing core blue for admin interfaces.

extends.name: color-action-primary
extends.description: The enterprise action color replaces the core primary blue with enterprise teal in admin interfaces. The core token's type, category, usage guidelines, and platform API mappings are inherited — only the resolved value changes.

modifications.description: Changed from '#0055b3' (core primary blue) to '#00796b' (enterprise teal).
All platform API mappings (CSS custom property, SCSS variable, JS constant, iOS/Android tokens) inherited from core.

useCases.description: When applying the primary action color to buttons, links, and interactive controls in enterprise admin interfaces.

alternative.name: color-action-primary
alternative.rationale: Consumer products use the core primary blue. The enterprise teal is specific to the admin sub-brand.

agents.intent: Apply the primary text color for body content, headings, and labels on default background surfaces.

constraints.rule: Do not use on dark or colored background surfaces.
Do not override this token value at the component level.

disambiguation.distinction: Use primary for default reading content; use secondary for supporting or reduced-emphasis text.
Use primary on default surfaces; use on-action inside filled interactive components.

metadata.value: semantic
Hover state for the enterprise action color.

agents.intent: Define and organize a set of related design tokens, providing shared defaults for type and category.

constraints.rule: Do not reference base palette tokens directly in component code.
constraints.context: Always reference semantic tokens that point to palette values.
